summary: Branch filtering refactoring verification completed. Backend: 12/12 tests passed. Frontend: Data properly filtered for restricted user (test@ssc.com). Admin sees all data, restricted user sees branch-specific + global items for suppliers/customers, and only branch-specific items for sales.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Branch filtering refactoring is complete and verified. Two helper functions in database.py: get_branch_filter() for strict branch filtering (sales/expenses) and get_branch_filter_with_global() for branch + global items (suppliers/customers). Restricted user test@ssc.com has permissions: dashboard:write, sales:write, invoices:write, customers:write, suppliers:read (but NO expenses permission).

suppliers_endpoint.details: Admin sees all 7 suppliers. Restricted user sees 6 (branch-specific + global)

customers_endpoint.details: Admin sees all 7 customers. Restricted user sees 6 (branch-specific + global)

sales_endpoint.details: Admin sees 47 sales from all branches. Restricted user sees only 15 from Test Branch

expenses_endpoint.details: Restricted user correctly denied (403) - no expenses permission in RBAC. Branch filtering works for users with permission.

dashboard_stats_endpoint.details: Restricted user sees filtered stats based on branch_id

frontend_data_segregation.details: UI correctly displays filtered data for both admin and restricted users

branch_filtering_verification.get_branch_filter: Returns {branch_id: user.branch_id} for restricted users. Used by sales.py and expenses.py. Restricts to user's branch ONLY.
branch_filtering_verification.get_branch_filter_with_global: Returns $or filter for branch-specific OR global (null/empty branch_id). Used by suppliers.py and customers.py. Includes global items for restricted users.
